title: Authors
text:
Names are surface forms. Author counts group by the exact name string in the source metadata. Two spellings of the same person (e.g. Newton, Isaac and Sir Isaac Newton) are counted separately. 1,643 articles (19%) have no listed author.
The catalogue records 2679 distinct author names across 7,638 author-article links.

title: Authors
text:
Career span vs. publication count
Each dot is an author. Hover for the name. The horizontal axis is the number of years between their first and last article in the catalogue (career span, not active years); the vertical axis is their total article count.

title: Authors
section: Timelines of the 30 most prolific authors
text:
Timelines of the 30 most prolific authors
One row per author; one marker per year in which they published. Marker size encodes how many articles in that year.

title: Authors
text:
How many authors per article?




                            
                                            


Figure 4




Single-author work dominates this period, with co-authored pieces remaining unusual until the late 19th century.

title: Length
text: Page length is computed as lastpage − firstpage + 1 for the 8,081 articles whose pagination is numeric. 464 articles (5.4%) — usually short front-matter with Roman-numeral pagination such as i, ii, iii — are excluded from this view.

title: Length
text:
Distribution of article lengths







        
        
        


(a)






                            
                                            


(b)





Figure 1




Short pieces dominate — over half of articles run to five pages or fewer.

title: Length
text:
Length composition by topic
For each of the top ten topics, the share of articles in each length bin.




                            
                                            


Figure 2




Letter and Errata skew very short; Article and Astronomical Observation are more bimodal, with a long tail of multi-dozen-page reports.

title: Length
text:
Median length over time




                            
                                            


Figure 3




Typical article length grows substantially across the 18th and 19th centuries — a few pages in the earliest years, more like a dozen in the late 1800s.

title: Philosophical Transactions Explorer
text: This site explores 8,545 articles from the Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society across 1665–1886 — counts by topic, author, and article length, plus how each of those changes over time.

title: Philosophical Transactions Explorer
text:
Where to start

Topics — distribution of the 25 topical labels, with the Experiment topic as a worked example.
Authors — the most prolific contributors, their career spans, and collaboration patterns.
Length — how page counts are distributed and how typical article length has changed over two centuries.
Dashboard — combine filters across year, topic, and author with live-updating charts.
Browse — every article, sortable and searchable, with links out to royalsocietypublishing.org.







What’s published



Pre-computed aggregates (counts by year, by topic, by author, by page length) and bibliographic metadata for every article (title, DOI, year, topic, author names, link to the publisher). The raw SQLite metadata file and the scraper’s internal URLs are not published. See About for the full statement.

title: Dashboard
text:
Code
topicYear = FileAttachment("data/derivatives/topic_year_counts.csv").csv({typed: true})
authorYear = FileAttachment("data/derivatives/author_year_counts_top50.csv").csv({typed: true})
articlesYear = FileAttachment("data/derivatives/articles_per_year.csv").csv({typed: true})
topicTotals = FileAttachment("data/derivatives/topic_totals.csv").csv({typed: true})
authorTotals = FileAttachment("data/derivatives/author_productivity.csv").csv({typed: true})







































Code
allTopics = [...new Set(topicYear.map(d => d.topic))].sort()
topAuthorNames = authorTotals.slice(0, 50).map(d => d.author_name)
yearExtent = d3.extent(topicYear, d => d.year)


























Filters


Code
viewof yearFrom = Inputs.range(yearExtent, {value: yearExtent[0], step: 1, label: "From year"})
viewof yearTo = Inputs.range(yearExtent, {value: yearExtent[1], step: 1, label: "To year"})
viewof selectedTopics = Inputs.select(allTopics, {
  multiple: 10,
  value: allTopics,
  label: "Topics"
})
viewof selectedAuthors = Inputs.select(["(all top-50)", ...topAuthorNames], {
  value: "(all top-50)",
  label: "Author"
})
































Code
filterYearRange = ({start: Math.min(yearFrom, yearTo), end: Math.max(yearFrom, yearTo)})
topicFiltered = topicYear.filter(d =>
  d.year >= filterYearRange.start &&
  d.year <= filterYearRange.end &&
  selectedTopics.includes(d.topic)
)
authorFiltered = authorYear.filter(d =>
  d.year >= filterYearRange.start &&
  d.year <= filterYearRange.end &&
  (selectedAuthors === "(all top-50)" || d.author_name === selectedAuthors)
)
filteredCount = d3.sum(topicFiltered, d => d.count)




































 articles match the current filter.





Articles per year — within the filter


Code
byYear = Array.from(
  d3.rollup(topicFiltered, v => d3.sum(v, d => d.count), d => d.year),
  ([year, count]) => ({year, count})
).sort((a, b) => a.year - b.year)

Plot.plot({
  height: 280,
  marginLeft: 50,
  x: {label: "Year", tickFormat: d => d.toString()},
  y: {label: "Articles", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.areaY(byYear, {x: "year", y: "count", fill: "#1f77b4", fillOpacity: 0.25}),
    Plot.lineY(byYear, {x: "year", y: "count", stroke: "#1f77b4", strokeWidth: 1.6}),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})




















Topics within the filter


Code
byTopic = Array.from(
  d3.rollup(topicFiltered, v => d3.sum(v, d => d.count), d => d.topic),
  ([topic, count]) => ({topic, count})
).sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)

Plot.plot({
  height: 360,
  marginLeft: 180,
  x: {label: "Articles", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, domain: byTopic.map(d => d.topic)},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(byTopic, {y: "topic", x: "count", fill: "#1f77b4"}),
    Plot.text(byTopic, {y: "topic", x: "count", text: d => d.count.toLocaleString(), dx: 4, textAnchor: "start"}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})


















Top-50 authors within the filter
If you’ve picked one author, only that author’s bar shows.


Code
byAuthor = Array.from(
  d3.rollup(authorFiltered, v => d3.sum(v, d => d.count), d => d.author_name),
  ([author, count]) => ({author, count})
).filter(d => d.count > 0)
  .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)
  .slice(0, 25)

Plot.plot({
  height: 360,
  marginLeft: 180,
  x: {label: "Articles", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, domain: byAuthor.map(d => d.author)},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(byAuthor, {y: "author", x: "count", fill: "#1f77b4"}),
    Plot.text(byAuthor, {y: "author", x: "count", text: d => d.count.toLocaleString(), dx: 4, textAnchor: "start"}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})

























What’s filterable, what isn’t






Year, Topic, and Author filters compose. Counts in the year and topic panels respect year + topic; the author panel respects year + author (it isn’t constrained by topic, because per-topic author counts aren’t pre-computed in this prototype).
Author filtering only knows about the 50 most-published authors.
Move to the Browse page for free-text search across every article.

title: About this site
text:
The Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society is the longest-running scientific journal in the world. Its first volume appeared in 1665, and its back catalogue offers a remarkable window onto more than two centuries of European natural philosophy and early science: experiments, astronomical observations, letters from correspondents, and reports from the Society’s meetings. The articles indexed by this site span 1665–1886 — 8,545 items across 25 broad topical labels.
As a part of the Secrets to Patent project, we have crawled the publicly-available metadata for these articles from royalsocietypublishing.org and assembled it into a single SQLite catalogue. This site uses pre-computed aggregates of that catalogue to make topic distributions explorable.
The site offers five views: topics, authors, article length, a cross-filter dashboard, and a sortable per-article browse table.

title: About this site
text:
What is and isn’t published
This site publishes:

Aggregate counts (articles per year, per topic, per topic × year).
Per-article bibliographic metadata for items in the focus view: title, DOI, year, topic, and author names — together with a link to the original article on royalsocietypublishing.org.

This site does not publish:

The raw SQLite metadata file.
Any internal paths or URLs

title: About this site
text:
A note on author names
Author counts are based on the surface form of the name as stored in the source metadata. No disambiguation is applied in this prototype, so the same person under two spellings (“Newton, Isaac” vs. “Sir Isaac Newton”) appears as two distinct authors. Roughly 19 % of articles have no listed author in the source.

title: About this site
text:
Team
Natacha Klein Käfer is the principal investigator. The crawl, database build, derivative pipeline, and Quarto site were assembled by Mathias Johansson using Python, SQLite, Quarto, Plotly, GitHub Pages, and Claude.
